reasons: Carmilla can link enemies so Terizla's AoE damage and control become more threatening in grouped fights.
Terizla benefits from enemies staying close enough for his heavy melee skills to hit multiple targets.

proof.summary: Carmilla links enemies in a grouped fight, while Terizla punishes the linked formation with slow, pull, and heavy AoE damage.
worksBestWhen: Carmilla lands Curse of Blood before Terizla uses Penalty Zone.
Terizla is close enough to follow the linked targets immediately.
failureCases: Enemies split apart and break the link.
Terizla casts before Carmilla confirms the link.
The team lacks follow-up damage after the control chain.

reasons: Atlas can pull multiple enemies into one place, giving Terizla a clear target area for his teamfight skills.
Terizla can follow Atlas' grouped control with a second layer of slow and AoE damage.

proof.summary: Atlas groups enemies with a hard engage, and Terizla follows with slow, pull, and heavy AoE punishment.
worksBestWhen: Terizla holds his ultimate until Atlas confirms the pull.
Terizla positions close enough before Atlas commits.
failureCases: Terizla casts too early before enemies are grouped.
Atlas catches targets outside Terizla's range.
The enemy spreads before Terizla arrives.

reasons: Tigreal can pull and stun enemies in one area, letting Terizla follow with slow, pull, and heavy AoE damage.
The combined control chain gives Terizla more reliable access despite his slower movement.

proof.summary: Tigreal starts a grouped crowd-control window, and Terizla follows with another AoE control and damage layer.
worksBestWhen: Terizla is already near the fight before Tigreal commits.
Tigreal catches enough targets for Terizla's AoE to matter.
failureCases: Tigreal engages too far away from Terizla.
Terizla is kited before reaching the controlled targets.
The enemy uses disengage after Tigreal's CC ends.

reasons: Mathilda helps compensate for Terizla's limited mobility by giving him a reposition path into or out of fights.
Terizla becomes more reliable when a teammate can help him access the fight.

proof.summary: Terizla is strong after reaching enemies, and Mathilda can improve his access or escape path around the fight.
worksBestWhen: Mathilda times the movement support before Terizla is kited.
Terizla still has key cooldowns after repositioning.
failureCases: Mathilda positions too far from the intended fight.

reasons: Floryn can keep Terizla alive while he commits into melee range and absorbs enemy focus.
Global healing supports Terizla during slow, commitment-heavy teamfight entries.

proof.summary: Terizla's engage is commitment-heavy, and Floryn can heal him globally during the focus window.
worksBestWhen: Floryn reacts when Terizla absorbs burst during his engage.
Terizla still has enough HP to continue after the heal.
failureCases: Terizla is crowd-controlled and killed before the heal matters.
Floryn uses Bloom before Terizla commits.
The enemy disengages after forcing Terizla's cooldowns.
